---
title: "_shinyplanr_ Manual"
subtitle: "A Guide to spatial prioritisation with _shinyplanr_"
author: "Jason D. Everett"
date: last-modified
date-format: "D MMMM YYYY"
---
 
<img src="images/logo.png" alt="shinyplanr logo" style="float: left; width: 100px; height: auto; margin-right: 15px; margin-bottom: 10px;">

Welcome to the ***shinyplanr*** manual. This guide provides comprehensive documentation for using and deploying *shinyplanr*, a Shiny application that provides an interactive graphical user interface for spatial prioritisation using the [*prioritizr*](https://prioritizr.net) R package.

<div style="clear: both;"></div>

## What is shinyplanr? {.unnumbered}

*shinyplanr* is a [*golem*](https://thinkr-open.github.io/golem/)-based Shiny application designed to make systematic conservation planning accessible to stakeholders, managers, and researchers. It allows users to:

- **Explore spatial planning scenarios** by adjusting conservation targets for biodiversity features
- **Compare different prioritisation outcomes** side-by-side
- **Incorporate real-world constraints** such as existing protected areas and areas unavailable for protection
- **Consider economic costs** to find efficient solutions
- **Evaluate climate-smart options** for robust, future-proof conservation networks
- **Download and share results** for use in reports and decision-making

## Who is this manual for? {.unnumbered}

This manual serves two primary audiences:

1. **End users** who interact with deployed *shinyplanr* applications to explore spatial planning scenarios for their region
2. **Semi-expert deployers** who set up and customise *shinyplanr* for new regions or new sets of planning options

## How to use this manual {.unnumbered}

- **Chapter 1** provides background on spatial prioritisation, climate-smart planning, multiple-use planning, and ecosystem services
- **Chapter 2** explains how to use the *shinyplanr* application interface
- **Chapter 3** guides deployers through creating a new *shinyplanr* instance for a region
- **Forthcoming:** Chapter 4 covers uploading your app to Posit Connect
- **Forthcoming:** Chapter 5 introduces helpful R packages for spatial planning workflows
- **Forthcoming:** Appendix A details software installation requirements
- **Forthcoming:** Appendix B introduces the MinPatch tool for minimum patch size constraints

## Acknowledgements {.unnumbered}

*shinyplanr* was developed by researchers at The University of Queensland. Development has been supported by various funding bodies and collaborators, in particular The Waitt Foundation.

If you use *shinyplanr* in your work, please cite:
 
> Everett, J.D., Richardson, A.J., Neubert, S. & Dunn, D.C. (2026). shinyplanr: Spatial Planning in Shiny. R package. https://github.com/SpatialPlanning/shinyplanr

## Getting help {.unnumbered}

If you encounter issues or have questions:

- Check the **Help** tab within the *shinyplanr* application
- Visit the [GitHub repository](https://github.com/SpatialPlanning/shinyplanr) to report bugs or request features
- Contact the development team

---

{{< include chapters/01-introduction.qmd >}}

{{< include chapters/02-using-shinyplanr.qmd >}}

{{< include chapters/03-setting-up.qmd >}}

<!-- {{< include chapters/04-deployment.qmd >}} -->

<!-- {{< include chapters/05-useful-tools.qmd >}} -->

<!-- {{< include chapters/appendix-a-software.qmd >}} -->

<!-- {{< include chapters/appendix-b-minpatch.qmd >}} -->

# References {.unnumbered}
